task_visualization.problem: Evaluate and diagnose the patient presenting with low back pain.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Evaluate and diagnose the patient presenting with low back pain.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient reports a sudden onset of severe lower back pain after lifting a heavy object 2 days ago. The pain is localized to the lower back without radiation. She denies any bowel or bladder incontinence. No previous history of similar episodes.

Secondary_Symptoms: Pain localized to the lumbar region
Pain onset after lifting a heavy object

Patient_Actor.Social_History: Non-smoker, does not consume alcohol. Works as a teacher.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies fever, weight loss, leg weakness, or numbness.

Musculoskeletal_Examination.Palpation: Localized tenderness over the lumbar spine, no palpable masses.
Musculoskeletal_Examination.Range_of_Motion: Limited due to pain, especially on forward flexion.
Musculoskeletal_Examination.Neurological_Examination: Normal, no signs of nerve root compression.

OSCE_Examination.Correct_Diagnosis: Displacement of lumbar intervertebral disc without myelopathy

patient_symptoms: Two days ago onset of low back pain after lifting a heavy object. Pain localized to the lower back; does not radiate to buttock or legs.

Location: central low back in the lumbar region, midline; sharp component and dull ache. Onset two days ago after lifting a heavy object. Persistent with no radiation to buttock/legs. Severity 6-7/10 at rest; up to 8/10 with bending or lifting. Mostly constant; worsens with movement; relieved by rest, heat, and mild analgesics.

Red flags: none; no fever, night sweats, weight loss, numbness/weakness in legs, bowel/bladder changes, gait disturbance, or night pain. Past history: no prior back problems; non-smoker, no alcohol; occupation: teacher with heavy lifting 2 days ago; OTC analgesics PRN; BMI not documented; family history negative for spine disease or cancer; pain midline lumbar region with sharp component and dull ache; 6-8/10; constant with movement; relieved by rest/heat.
Current pain medications: ibuprofen 400 mg Q6h PRN; acetaminophen 500 mg Q6h PRN; no known drug allergies or NSAIDs intolerance.
physical_exmination_findings: Posture/gait: Not documented. Inspection: No visible deformities or bruising. Palpation: Localized tenderness over lumbar spine; paraspinal region tenderness. ROM: Limited due to pain, esp forward flexion. Localized lumbar tenderness present. SLR: Not documented. Lower extremity neuro exam: Normal; no signs of nerve root compression.
test_results: X-ray lumbar spine: ordered; result pending.
Non-contrast lumbar spine MRI: test unavailable.
osce_note.differential_diagnosis_list: Acute nonspecific low back pain; Lumbar strain; Vertebral compression fracture

dialogue_history: Doctor: When did your low back pain start, and is it localized to the lower back or does it radiate to the buttock or legs?
Patient: Two days ago I started having this pain after lifting a heavy object. It’s localized to my lower back and does not radiate to my buttock or legs.

Doctor: Please describe: exact location and quality of pain (e.g., dull, sharp, aching); onset and duration; any radiation to buttock or legs; pain severity 0-10; constant or intermittent; factors that worsen or relieve it (movement, rest, lifting).

Patient: Location and quality: central low back in the lumbar region, around the midline with a sharp component and a dull ache. Onset was two days ago after lifting a heavy object; the pain has been persistent with no radiation to buttock or legs. Severity is about 6-7/10 at rest and up to 8/10 with bending or lifting; it is mostly constant and worsens with movement, relieved by rest, heat, and mild analgesics.

Doctor: Please describe: (1) red flags (fever/night sweats, unintended weight loss, numbness/weakness in legs, bowel/bladder changes); (2) history of prior back problems, osteoporosis, cancer; (3) current medications and allergies; (4) smoking, alcohol, BMI; (5) occupation and any recent heavy lifting or prolonged awkward postures; (6) family history of spine disease or cancer; (7) any night pain or pain at rest; (8) any gait changes; (9) other factors you think are important.
Doctor_Uncertainty: Acute nonspecific low back pain; Lumbar strain; Vertebral compression fracture

Patient: Red flags: none—no fever, night sweats, unintended weight loss, numbness or weakness in the legs, bowel/bladder changes, gait disturbance, or night pain. Past history: no prior back problems; non-smoker, no alcohol; occupation is teacher with a heavy lift 2 days ago; currently using OTC analgesics as needed with no known allergies; BMI not documented; family history negative for spine disease or cancer; pain is midline in the lumbar region with a sharp component and dull ache, 6–8/10, constant with movement and relieved by rest/heat.

Doctor: REQUEST TEST: Please perform a focused lumbar spine physical examination: inspect posture and gait, palpate paraspinal muscles, assess range of motion (flexion/extension/lateral bending), check for localized tenderness, perform straight leg raise tests, and do a basic lower extremity neurologic exam (strength, sensation, reflexes).
Doctor_Uncertainty: Acute nonspecific low back pain; Lumbar strain; Vertebral compression fracture

Measurement: RESULTS: - Posture/gait: Not documented in available data. - Inspection: No visible deformities or bruising. - Palpation: Localized tenderness over the lumbar spine; paraspinal region tenderness noted. - Range of motion: Limited due to pain, especially on forward flexion. - Localized tenderness: Present at the lumbar spine. - Straight leg raise: Not documented/unavailable. - Lower extremity neurologic exam: Normal; no signs of nerve root compression. (Strength, sensation, and reflexes not detailed beyond overall normal exam.)
Doctor: REQUEST TEST: X-ray lumbar spine
Doctor_Uncertainty: Acute nonspecific low back pain; Lumbar strain; Vertebral compression fracture

Measurement: RESULTS: Focused lumbar spine examination: - Posture/gait: Not documented in available data. - Inspection: No visible deformities or bruising. - Palpation: Localized tenderness over the lumbar spine; paraspinal region tenderness noted. - Range of motion: Limited due to pain, especially on forward flexion. - Localized tenderness: Present at the lumbar spine. - Straight leg raise: Not documented/unavailable. - Lower extremity neurologic exam: Normal; no signs of nerve root compression. (Strength, sensation, and reflexes not detailed beyond overall normal exam.) - X-ray lumbar spine: Test unavailable in current data.
Doctor: REQUEST TEST: Please perform and document the straight leg raise test results, including whether pain is reproduced and any differences between legs.

Measurement: RESULTS: Straight leg raise test results: Test unavailable in current data; unable to assess reproduction of pain or differences between legs.

agent_visualization.agent_system_prompt:
STATIC MEMORY
{
  "system_prompt": "You are an AgentClinic information-gathering clinician. Your goal is to collect enough clinical information to support diagnosis by interacting with the hospital environment and maintaining a concise OSCE note.

Use these tools:
- `update_osce_note`: update the OSCE note after every new patient response, physical examination result, or test result.
  - Use section `differential_diagnosis_list` for the current top 3 most likely diagnoses.
  - Format `differential_diagnosis_list` content as one string separated by semicolons, for example: `Acute appendicitis; Gastroenteritis; Ovarian torsion`.
- `bash`: call `src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py` for AgentClinic actions:
  - `respond`: interact with the hospital environment using one action: `ask_patient`, `request_physical_examination`, or `request_test`.

Call AgentClinic actions through the `bash` tool with `uv run python`. Use this command shape:

```bash
# Ask the patient one focused clinical question.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"ask_patient","message":"When did the abdominal pain start, and where is it located now?"}}'
```

```bash
# Request one clinically useful test from the hospital environment.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_test","message":"Complete blood count"}}'
```

```bash
# Request one focused physical examination result from the hospital environment.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_physical_examination","message":"Abdominal examination"}}'
```

Workflow:
1. Keep a working differential diagnosis in mind while asking questions and requesting exams/tests.
2. Ask clinically useful questions that build a comprehensive picture: chief concern, history of present illness, associated symptoms, negatives, past history, medications, allergies, family/social history, and relevant risk factors.
3. Request focused physical examinations and tests only when they can meaningfully narrow the differential or assess severity.
4. After each hospital environment response, immediately update the OSCE note with the new information.
5. When having enough context about the patient or the likely diagnoses change, update OSCE section `differential_diagnosis_list` with the latest top 3 diagnoses separated by `;`.

Avoid:
- Do not get stuck on overly detailed points before covering the main clinical picture.
- Do not repeat the same or very similar question, examination, or test request.
- If an examination or test result is unavailable, move on and try a different useful action.
- Do not ask the patient for information that was already provided unless clarification is necessary.

Use `respond` for the next hospital interaction. Do not provide a final diagnosis during this information-gathering phase.
",
  "memory_object": {
    "plan": [],
    "evidence": [],
    "osce_note": {
      "patient_demographics": [],
      "patient_medical_history": [],
      "patient_social_history": [],
      "patient_symptoms": [],
      "physical_exmination_findings": [],
      "test_results": [],
      "differential_diagnosis_list": ""
    }
  }
}

DYNAMIC MEMORY
The appended agent trajectory follows as subsequent messages.

{

Use these tools:
- `update_osce_note`: update the OSCE note after every new patient response, physical examination result, or test result.
  - Use section `differential_diagnosis_list` for the current top 3 most likely diagnoses.
  - Format `differential_diagnosis_list` content as one string separated by semicolons, for example: `Acute appendicitis; Gastroenteritis; Ovarian torsion`.
- `bash`: call `src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py` for AgentClinic actions:
  - `respond`: interact with the hospital environment using one action: `ask_patient`, `request_physical_examination`, or `request_test`.

Call AgentClinic actions through the `bash` tool with `uv run python`. Use this command shape:

```bash
# Ask the patient one focused clinical question.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"ask_patient","message":"When did the abdominal pain start, and where is it located now?"}}'
```

```bash
# Request one clinically useful test from the hospital environment.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_test","message":"Complete blood count"}}'
```

```bash
# Request one focused physical examination result from the hospital environment.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_physical_examination","message":"Abdominal examination"}}'
```

Workflow:
1. Keep a working differential diagnosis in mind while asking questions and requesting exams/tests.
2. Ask clinically useful questions that build a comprehensive picture: chief concern, history of present illness, associated symptoms, negatives, past history, medications, allergies, family/social history, and relevant risk factors.
3. Request focused physical examinations and tests only when they can meaningfully narrow the differential or assess severity.
4. After each hospital environment response, immediately update the OSCE note with the new information.
5. When having enough context about the patient or the likely diagnoses change, update OSCE section `differential_diagnosis_list` with the latest top 3 diagnoses separated by `;`.

Avoid:
- Do not get stuck on overly detailed points before covering the main clinical picture.
- Do not repeat the same or very similar question, examination, or test request.
- If an examination or test result is unavailable, move on and try a different useful action.
- Do not ask the patient for information that was already provided unless clarification is necessary.

Use `respond` for the next hospital interaction. Do not provide a final diagnosis during this information-gathering phase.
",

STATIC MEMORY
{
  "system_prompt": "You are an AgentClinic information-gathering clinician. Your goal is to collect enough clinical information to support diagnosis by interacting with the hospital environment and maintaining a concise OSCE note.

Use these tools:
- `update_osce_note`: update the OSCE note after every new patient response, physical examination result, or test result.
  - Use section `differential_diagnosis_list` for the current top 3 most likely diagnoses.
  - Format `differential_diagnosis_list` content as one string separated by semicolons, for example: `Acute appendicitis; Gastroenteritis; Ovarian torsion`.
- `bash`: call `src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py` for AgentClinic actions:
  - `respond`: interact with the hospital environment using one action: `ask_patient`, `request_physical_examination`, or `request_test`.

Call AgentClinic actions through the `bash` tool with `uv run python`. Use this command shape:

```bash
# Ask the patient one focused clinical question.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"ask_patient","message":"When did the abdominal pain start, and where is it located now?"}}'
```

```bash
# Request one clinically useful test from the hospital environment.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_test","message":"Complete blood count"}}'
```

```bash
# Request one focused physical examination result from the hospital environment.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_physical_examination","message":"Abdominal examination"}}'
```

Workflow:
1. Keep a working differential diagnosis in mind while asking questions and requesting exams/tests.
2. Ask clinically useful questions that build a comprehensive picture: chief concern, history of present illness, associated symptoms, negatives, past history, medications, allergies, family/social history, and relevant risk factors.
3. Request focused physical examinations and tests only when they can meaningfully narrow the differential or assess severity.
4. After each hospital environment response, immediately update the OSCE note with the new information.
5. When having enough context about the patient or the likely diagnoses change, update OSCE section `differential_diagnosis_list` with the latest top 3 diagnoses separated by `;`.

Avoid:
- Do not get stuck on overly detailed points before covering the main clinical picture.
- Do not repeat the same or very similar question, examination, or test request.
- If an examination or test result is unavailable, move on and try a different useful action.
- Do not ask the patient for information that was already provided unless clarification is necessary.

Use `respond` for the next hospital interaction. Do not provide a final diagnosis during this information-gathering phase.
",
  "memory_object": {
    "plan": [],
    "evidence": [],
    "osce_note": {
      "patient_demographics": [],
      "patient_medical_history": [],
      "patient_social_history": [],

        "Location: central low back in the lumbar region, midline; sharp component and dull ache. Onset two days ago after lifting a heavy object. Persistent with no radiation to buttock/legs. Severity 6-7/10 at rest; up to 8/10 with bending or lifting. Mostly constant; worsens with movement; relieved by rest, heat, and mild analgesics.",
        "Red flags: none; no fever, night sweats, weight loss, numbness/weakness in legs, bowel/bladder changes, gait disturbance, or night pain. Past history: no prior back problems; non-smoker, no alcohol; occupation: teacher with heavy lifting 2 days ago; OTC analgesics PRN; BMI not documented; family history negative for spine disease or cancer; pain midline lumbar region with sharp component and dull ache; 6-8/10; constant with movement; relieved by rest/heat."
      ],
      "physical_exmination_findings": [],
      "test_results": [],
      "differential_diagnosis_list": "Acute nonspecific low back pain; Lumbar strain; Vertebral compression fracture"
    }
  }
}

STATIC MEMORY
{
  "system_prompt": "You are an AgentClinic information-gathering clinician. Your goal is to collect enough clinical information to support diagnosis by interacting with the hospital environment and maintaining a concise OSCE note.

Use these tools:
- `update_osce_note`: update the OSCE note after every new patient response, physical examination result, or test result.
  - Use section `differential_diagnosis_list` for the current top 3 most likely diagnoses.
  - Format `differential_diagnosis_list` content as one string separated by semicolons, for example: `Acute appendicitis; Gastroenteritis; Ovarian torsion`.
- `bash`: call `src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py` for AgentClinic actions:
  - `respond`: interact with the hospital environment using one action: `ask_patient`, `request_physical_examination`, or `request_test`.

Call AgentClinic actions through the `bash` tool with `uv run python`. Use this command shape:

```bash
# Ask the patient one focused clinical question.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"ask_patient","message":"When did the abdominal pain start, and where is it located now?"}}'
```

```bash
# Request one clinically useful test from the hospital environment.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_test","message":"Complete blood count"}}'
```

```bash
# Request one focused physical examination result from the hospital environment.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_physical_examination","message":"Abdominal examination"}}'
```

Workflow:
1. Keep a working differential diagnosis in mind while asking questions and requesting exams/tests.
2. Ask clinically useful questions that build a comprehensive picture: chief concern, history of present illness, associated symptoms, negatives, past history, medications, allergies, family/social history, and relevant risk factors.
3. Request focused physical examinations and tests only when they can meaningfully narrow the differential or assess severity.
4. After each hospital environment response, immediately update the OSCE note with the new information.
5. When having enough context about the patient or the likely diagnoses change, update OSCE section `differential_diagnosis_list` with the latest top 3 diagnoses separated by `;`.

Avoid:
- Do not get stuck on overly detailed points before covering the main clinical picture.
- Do not repeat the same or very similar question, examination, or test request.
- If an examination or test result is unavailable, move on and try a different useful action.
- Do not ask the patient for information that was already provided unless clarification is necessary.

Use `respond` for the next hospital interaction. Do not provide a final diagnosis during this information-gathering phase.

{
  "system_prompt": "You are an AgentClinic information-gathering clinician. Your goal is to collect enough clinical information to support diagnosis by interacting with the hospital environment and maintaining a concise OSCE note.

Use these tools:
- `update_osce_note`: update the OSCE note after every new patient response, physical examination result, or test result.
  - Use section `differential_diagnosis_list` for the current top 3 most likely diagnoses.
  - Format `differential_diagnosis_list` content as one string separated by semicolons, for example: `Acute appendicitis; Gastroenteritis; Ovarian torsion`.
- `bash`: call `src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py` for AgentClinic actions:
  - `respond`: interact with the hospital environment using one action: `ask_patient`, `request_physical_examination`, or `request_test`.

Call AgentClinic actions through the `bash` tool with `uv run python`. Use this command shape:

```bash
# Ask the patient one focused clinical question.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"ask_patient","message":"When did the abdominal pain start, and where is it located now?"}}'
```

```bash
# Request one clinically useful test from the hospital environment.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_test","message":"Complete blood count"}}'
```

```bash
# Request one focused physical examination result from the hospital environment.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_physical_examination","message":"Abdominal examination"}}'
```

Workflow:
1. Keep a working differential diagnosis in mind while asking questions and requesting exams/tests.
2. Ask clinically useful questions that build a comprehensive picture: chief concern, history of present illness, associated symptoms, negatives, past history, medications, allergies, family/social history, and relevant risk factors.
3. Request focused physical examinations and tests only when they can meaningfully narrow the differential or assess severity.
4. After each hospital environment response, immediately update the OSCE note with the new information.
5. When having enough context about the patient or the likely diagnoses change, update OSCE section `differential_diagnosis_list` with the latest top 3 diagnoses separated by `;`.

Avoid:
- Do not get stuck on overly detailed points before covering the main clinical picture.
- Do not repeat the same or very similar question, examination, or test request.
- If an examination or test result is unavailable, move on and try a different useful action.
- Do not ask the patient for information that was already provided unless clarification is necessary.

Use `respond` for the next hospital interaction. Do not provide a final diagnosis during this information-gathering phase.

STATIC MEMORY
{
  "system_prompt": "You are an AgentClinic information-gathering clinician. Your goal is to collect enough clinical information to support diagnosis by interacting with the hospital environment and maintaining a concise OSCE note.

Use these tools:
- `update_osce_note`: update the OSCE note after every new patient response, physical examination result, or test result.
  - Use section `differential_diagnosis_list` for the current top 3 most likely diagnoses.
  - Format `differential_diagnosis_list` content as one string separated by semicolons, for example: `Acute appendicitis; Gastroenteritis; Ovarian torsion`.
- `bash`: call `src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py` for AgentClinic actions:
  - `respond`: interact with the hospital environment using one action: `ask_patient`, `request_physical_examination`, or `request_test`.

Call AgentClinic actions through the `bash` tool with `uv run python`. Use this command shape:

```bash
# Ask the patient one focused clinical question.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"ask_patient","message":"When did the abdominal pain start, and where is it located now?"}}'
```

```bash
# Request one clinically useful test from the hospital environment.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_test","message":"Complete blood count"}}'
```

```bash
# Request one focused physical examination result from the hospital environment.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_physical_examination","message":"Abdominal examination"}}'
```

Workflow:
1. Keep a working differential diagnosis in mind while asking questions and requesting exams/tests.
2. Ask clinically useful questions that build a comprehensive picture: chief concern, history of present illness, associated symptoms, negatives, past history, medications, allergies, family/social history, and relevant risk factors.
3. Request focused physical examinations and tests only when they can meaningfully narrow the differential or assess severity.
4. After each hospital environment response, immediately update the OSCE note with the new information.
5. When having enough context about the patient or the likely diagnoses change, update OSCE section `differential_diagnosis_list` with the latest top 3 diagnoses separated by `;`.

Avoid:
- Do not get stuck on overly detailed points before covering the main clinical picture.
- Do not repeat the same or very similar question, examination, or test request.
- If an examination or test result is unavailable, move on and try a different useful action.
- Do not ask the patient for information that was already provided unless clarification is necessary.

Use `respond` for the next hospital interaction. Do not provide a final diagnosis during this information-gathering phase.
",

Severity 6-7/10 at rest; up to 8/10 with bending or lifting. Mostly constant; worsens with movement; relieved by rest, heat, and mild analgesics.",
        "Red flags: none; no fever, night sweats, weight loss, numbness/weakness in legs, bowel/bladder changes, gait disturbance, or night pain. Past history: no prior back problems; non-smoker, no alcohol; occupation: teacher with heavy lifting 2 days ago; OTC analgesics PRN; BMI not documented; family history negative for spine disease or cancer; pain midline lumbar region with sharp component and dull ache; 6-8/10; constant with movement; relieved by rest/heat."
      ],
      "physical_exmination_findings": [

ROM: Limited due to pain, esp forward flexion. Localized lumbar tenderness present. SLR: Not documented. Lower extremity neuro exam: Normal; no signs of nerve root compression."
      ],
      "test_results": [

}

STATIC MEMORY
{
  "system_prompt": "You are an AgentClinic information-gathering clinician. Your goal is to collect enough clinical information to support diagnosis by interacting with the hospital environment and maintaining a concise OSCE note.

Use these tools:
- `update_osce_note`: update the OSCE note after every new patient response, physical examination result, or test result.
  - Use section `differential_diagnosis_list` for the current top 3 most likely diagnoses.
  - Format `differential_diagnosis_list` content as one string separated by semicolons, for example: `Acute appendicitis; Gastroenteritis; Ovarian torsion`.
- `bash`: call `src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py` for AgentClinic actions:
  - `respond`: interact with the hospital environment using one action: `ask_patient`, `request_physical_examination`, or `request_test`.

Call AgentClinic actions through the `bash` tool with `uv run python`. Use this command shape:

```bash
# Ask the patient one focused clinical question.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"ask_patient","message":"When did the abdominal pain start, and where is it located now?"}}'
```

```bash
# Request one clinically useful test from the hospital environment.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_test","message":"Complete blood count"}}'
```

```bash
# Request one focused physical examination result from the hospital environment.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_physical_examination","message":"Abdominal examination"}}'
```

Workflow:
1. Keep a working differential diagnosis in mind while asking questions and requesting exams/tests.
2. Ask clinically useful questions that build a comprehensive picture: chief concern, history of present illness, associated symptoms, negatives, past history, medications, allergies, family/social history, and relevant risk factors.
3. Request focused physical examinations and tests only when they can meaningfully narrow the differential or assess severity.
4. After each hospital environment response, immediately update the OSCE note with the new information.
5. When having enough context about the patient or the likely diagnoses change, update OSCE section `differential_diagnosis_list` with the latest top 3 diagnoses separated by `;`.

Avoid:
- Do not get stuck on overly detailed points before covering the main clinical picture.
- Do not repeat the same or very similar question, examination, or test request.
- If an examination or test result is unavailable, move on and try a different useful action.
- Do not ask the patient for information that was already provided unless clarification is necessary.

Use `respond` for the next hospital interaction. Do not provide a final diagnosis during this information-gathering phase.
",

    "evidence": [],

      "patient_demographics": [],

        "Two days ago onset of low back pain after lifting a heavy object. Pain localized to the lower back; does not radiate to buttock or legs.",
        "Location: central low back in the lumbar region, midline; sharp component and dull ache. Onset two days ago after lifting a heavy object. Persistent with no radiation to buttock/legs. Severity 6-7/10 at rest; up to 8/10 with bending or lifting. Mostly constant; worsens with movement; relieved by rest, heat, and mild analgesics.",
        "Red flags: none; no fever, night sweats, weight loss, numbness/weakness in legs, bowel/bladder changes, gait disturbance, or night pain. Past history: no prior back problems; non-smoker, no alcohol; occupation: teacher with heavy lifting 2 days ago; OTC analgesics PRN; BMI not documented; family history negative for spine disease or cancer; pain midline lumbar region with sharp component and dull ache; 6-8/10; constant with movement; relieved by rest/heat."
      ],
      "physical_exmination_findings": [
        "Posture/gait: Not documented. Inspection: No visible deformities or bruising. Palpation: Localized tenderness over lumbar spine; paraspinal region tenderness. ROM: Limited due to pain, esp forward flexion. Localized lumbar tenderness present. SLR: Not documented. Lower extremity neuro exam: Normal; no signs of nerve root compression."
      ],
      "test_results": [
        "X-ray lumbar spine: ordered; result pending."
      ],
      "differential_diagnosis_list": "Acute nonspecific low back pain; Lumbar strain; Vertebral compression fracture"
    }

}

STATIC MEMORY
{
  "system_prompt": "You are an AgentClinic information-gathering clinician. Your goal is to collect enough clinical information to support diagnosis by interacting with the hospital environment and maintaining a concise OSCE note.

Use these tools:
- `update_osce_note`: update the OSCE note after every new patient response, physical examination result, or test result.
  - Use section `differential_diagnosis_list` for the current top 3 most likely diagnoses.
  - Format `differential_diagnosis_list` content as one string separated by semicolons, for example: `Acute appendicitis; Gastroenteritis; Ovarian torsion`.
- `bash`: call `src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py` for AgentClinic actions:
  - `respond`: interact with the hospital environment using one action: `ask_patient`, `request_physical_examination`, or `request_test`.

Call AgentClinic actions through the `bash` tool with `uv run python`. Use this command shape:

```bash
# Ask the patient one focused clinical question.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"ask_patient","message":"When did the abdominal pain start, and where is it located now?"}}'
```

```bash
# Request one clinically useful test from the hospital environment.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_test","message":"Complete blood count"}}'
```

```bash
# Request one focused physical examination result from the hospital environment.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_physical_examination","message":"Abdominal examination"}}'
```

Workflow:
1. Keep a working differential diagnosis in mind while asking questions and requesting exams/tests.
2. Ask clinically useful questions that build a comprehensive picture: chief concern, history of present illness, associated symptoms, negatives, past history, medications, allergies, family/social history, and relevant risk factors.
3. Request focused physical examinations and tests only when they can meaningfully narrow the differential or assess severity.
4. After each hospital environment response, immediately update the OSCE note with the new information.
5. When having enough context about the patient or the likely diagnoses change, update OSCE section `differential_diagnosis_list` with the latest top 3 diagnoses separated by `;`.

Avoid:
- Do not get stuck on overly detailed points before covering the main clinical picture.
- Do not repeat the same or very similar question, examination, or test request.
- If an examination or test result is unavailable, move on and try a different useful action.
- Do not ask the patient for information that was already provided unless clarification is necessary.

Use `respond` for the next hospital interaction. Do not provide a final diagnosis during this information-gathering phase.
",

Severity 6-7/10 at rest; up to 8/10 with bending or lifting. Mostly constant; worsens with movement; relieved by rest, heat, and mild analgesics.",
        "Red flags: none; no fever, night sweats, weight loss, numbness/weakness in legs, bowel/bladder changes, gait disturbance, or night pain. Past history: no prior back problems; non-smoker, no alcohol; occupation: teacher with heavy lifting 2 days ago; OTC analgesics PRN; BMI not documented; family history negative for spine disease or cancer; pain midline lumbar region with sharp component and dull ache; 6-8/10; constant with movement; relieved by rest/heat."
      ],
      "physical_exmination_findings": [
        "Posture/gait: Not documented. Inspection: No visible deformities or bruising. Palpation: Localized tenderness over lumbar spine; paraspinal region tenderness. ROM: Limited due to pain, esp forward flexion. Localized lumbar tenderness present. SLR: Not documented. Lower extremity neuro exam: Normal; no signs of nerve root compression.",
        "SLR: Not documented/unavailable."

}

STATIC MEMORY
{
  "system_prompt": "You are an AgentClinic information-gathering clinician. Your goal is to collect enough clinical information to support diagnosis by interacting with the hospital environment and maintaining a concise OSCE note.

Use these tools:
- `update_osce_note`: update the OSCE note after every new patient response, physical examination result, or test result.
  - Use section `differential_diagnosis_list` for the current top 3 most likely diagnoses.
  - Format `differential_diagnosis_list` content as one string separated by semicolons, for example: `Acute appendicitis; Gastroenteritis; Ovarian torsion`.
- `bash`: call `src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py` for AgentClinic actions:
  - `respond`: interact with the hospital environment using one action: `ask_patient`, `request_physical_examination`, or `request_test`.

Call AgentClinic actions through the `bash` tool with `uv run python`. Use this command shape:

```bash
# Ask the patient one focused clinical question.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"ask_patient","message":"When did the abdominal pain start, and where is it located now?"}}'
```

```bash
# Request one clinically useful test from the hospital environment.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_test","message":"Complete blood count"}}'
```

```bash
# Request one focused physical examination result from the hospital environment.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_physical_examination","message":"Abdominal examination"}}'
```

Workflow:
1. Keep a working differential diagnosis in mind while asking questions and requesting exams/tests.
2. Ask clinically useful questions that build a comprehensive picture: chief concern, history of present illness, associated symptoms, negatives, past history, medications, allergies, family/social history, and relevant risk factors.
3. Request focused physical examinations and tests only when they can meaningfully narrow the differential or assess severity.
4. After each hospital environment response, immediately update the OSCE note with the new information.
5. When having enough context about the patient or the likely diagnoses change, update OSCE section `differential_diagnosis_list` with the latest top 3 diagnoses separated by `;`.

Avoid:
- Do not get stuck on overly detailed points before covering the main clinical picture.
- Do not repeat the same or very similar question, examination, or test request.
- If an examination or test result is unavailable, move on and try a different useful action.
- Do not ask the patient for information that was already provided unless clarification is necessary.

Use `respond` for the next hospital interaction. Do not provide a final diagnosis during this information-gathering phase.
",
  "memory_object": {
    "plan": [],
    "evidence": [],
    "osce_note": {
      "patient_demographics": [],
      "patient_medical_history": [],
      "patient_social_history": [],

        "Two days ago onset of low back pain after lifting a heavy object. Pain localized to the lower back; does not radiate to buttock or legs.",
        "Location: central low back in the lumbar region, midline; sharp component and dull ache. Onset two days ago after lifting a heavy object. Persistent with no radiation to buttock/legs. Severity 6-7/10 at rest; up to 8/10 with bending or lifting. Mostly constant; worsens with movement; relieved by rest, heat, and mild analgesics.",
        "Red flags: none; no fever, night sweats, weight loss, numbness/weakness in legs, bowel/bladder changes, gait disturbance, or night pain. Past history: no prior back problems; non-smoker, no alcohol; occupation: teacher with heavy lifting 2 days ago; OTC analgesics PRN; BMI not documented; family history negative for spine disease or cancer; pain midline lumbar region with sharp component and dull ache; 6-8/10; constant with movement; relieved by rest/heat."
      ],
      "physical_exmination_findings": [
        "Posture/gait: Not documented. Inspection: No visible deformities or bruising. Palpation: Localized tenderness over lumbar spine; paraspinal region tenderness. ROM: Limited due to pain, esp forward flexion. Localized lumbar tenderness present. SLR: Not documented. Lower extremity neuro exam: Normal; no signs of nerve root compression.",
        "SLR: Not documented/unavailable."
      ],
      "test_results": [
        "X-ray lumbar spine: ordered; result pending."
      ],
      "differential_diagnosis_list": "Acute nonspecific low back pain; Lumbar strain; Vertebral compression fracture"
    }
  }
}

STATIC MEMORY
{
  "system_prompt": "You are an AgentClinic information-gathering clinician. Your goal is to collect enough clinical information to support diagnosis by interacting with the hospital environment and maintaining a concise OSCE note.

Use these tools:
- `update_osce_note`: update the OSCE note after every new patient response, physical examination result, or test result.
  - Use section `differential_diagnosis_list` for the current top 3 most likely diagnoses.
  - Format `differential_diagnosis_list` content as one string separated by semicolons, for example: `Acute appendicitis; Gastroenteritis; Ovarian torsion`.
- `bash`: call `src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py` for AgentClinic actions:
  - `respond`: interact with the hospital environment using one action: `ask_patient`, `request_physical_examination`, or `request_test`.

Call AgentClinic actions through the `bash` tool with `uv run python`. Use this command shape:

```bash
# Ask the patient one focused clinical question.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"ask_patient","message":"When did the abdominal pain start, and where is it located now?"}}'
```

```bash
# Request one clinically useful test from the hospital environment.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_test","message":"Complete blood count"}}'
```

```bash
# Request one focused physical examination result from the hospital environment.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_physical_examination","message":"Abdominal examination"}}'
```

Workflow:
1. Keep a working differential diagnosis in mind while asking questions and requesting exams/tests.
2. Ask clinically useful questions that build a comprehensive picture: chief concern, history of present illness, associated symptoms, negatives, past history, medications, allergies, family/social history, and relevant risk factors.
3. Request focused physical examinations and tests only when they can meaningfully narrow the differential or assess severity.
4. After each hospital environment response, immediately update the OSCE note with the new information.
5. When having enough context about the patient or the likely diagnoses change, update OSCE section `differential_diagnosis_list` with the latest top 3 diagnoses separated by `;`.

Avoid:
- Do not get stuck on overly detailed points before covering the main clinical picture.
- Do not repeat the same or very similar question, examination, or test request.
- If an examination or test result is unavailable, move on and try a different useful action.
- Do not ask the patient for information that was already provided unless clarification is necessary.

Use `respond` for the next hospital interaction. Do not provide a final diagnosis during this information-gathering phase.

{
  "system_prompt": "You are an AgentClinic information-gathering clinician. Your goal is to collect enough clinical information to support diagnosis by interacting with the hospital environment and maintaining a concise OSCE note.

Use these tools:
- `update_osce_note`: update the OSCE note after every new patient response, physical examination result, or test result.
  - Use section `differential_diagnosis_list` for the current top 3 most likely diagnoses.
  - Format `differential_diagnosis_list` content as one string separated by semicolons, for example: `Acute appendicitis; Gastroenteritis; Ovarian torsion`.
- `bash`: call `src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py` for AgentClinic actions:
  - `respond`: interact with the hospital environment using one action: `ask_patient`, `request_physical_examination`, or `request_test`.

Call AgentClinic actions through the `bash` tool with `uv run python`. Use this command shape:

```bash
# Ask the patient one focused clinical question.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"ask_patient","message":"When did the abdominal pain start, and where is it located now?"}}'
```

```bash
# Request one clinically useful test from the hospital environment.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_test","message":"Complete blood count"}}'
```

```bash
# Request one focused physical examination result from the hospital environment.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_physical_examination","message":"Abdominal examination"}}'
```

Workflow:
1. Keep a working differential diagnosis in mind while asking questions and requesting exams/tests.
2. Ask clinically useful questions that build a comprehensive picture: chief concern, history of present illness, associated symptoms, negatives, past history, medications, allergies, family/social history, and relevant risk factors.
3. Request focused physical examinations and tests only when they can meaningfully narrow the differential or assess severity.
4. After each hospital environment response, immediately update the OSCE note with the new information.
5. When having enough context about the patient or the likely diagnoses change, update OSCE section `differential_diagnosis_list` with the latest top 3 diagnoses separated by `;`.

Avoid:
- Do not get stuck on overly detailed points before covering the main clinical picture.
- Do not repeat the same or very similar question, examination, or test request.
- If an examination or test result is unavailable, move on and try a different useful action.
- Do not ask the patient for information that was already provided unless clarification is necessary.

Use `respond` for the next hospital interaction. Do not provide a final diagnosis during this information-gathering phase.

STATIC MEMORY
{
  "system_prompt": "You are an AgentClinic information-gathering clinician. Your goal is to collect enough clinical information to support diagnosis by interacting with the hospital environment and maintaining a concise OSCE note.

Use these tools:
- `update_osce_note`: update the OSCE note after every new patient response, physical examination result, or test result.
  - Use section `differential_diagnosis_list` for the current top 3 most likely diagnoses.
  - Format `differential_diagnosis_list` content as one string separated by semicolons, for example: `Acute appendicitis; Gastroenteritis; Ovarian torsion`.
- `bash`: call `src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py` for AgentClinic actions:
  - `respond`: interact with the hospital environment using one action: `ask_patient`, `request_physical_examination`, or `request_test`.

Call AgentClinic actions through the `bash` tool with `uv run python`. Use this command shape:

```bash
# Ask the patient one focused clinical question.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"ask_patient","message":"When did the abdominal pain start, and where is it located now?"}}'
```

```bash
# Request one clinically useful test from the hospital environment.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_test","message":"Complete blood count"}}'
```

```bash
# Request one focused physical examination result from the hospital environment.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_physical_examination","message":"Abdominal examination"}}'
```

Workflow:
1. Keep a working differential diagnosis in mind while asking questions and requesting exams/tests.
2. Ask clinically useful questions that build a comprehensive picture: chief concern, history of present illness, associated symptoms, negatives, past history, medications, allergies, family/social history, and relevant risk factors.
3. Request focused physical examinations and tests only when they can meaningfully narrow the differential or assess severity.
4. After each hospital environment response, immediately update the OSCE note with the new information.
5. When having enough context about the patient or the likely diagnoses change, update OSCE section `differential_diagnosis_list` with the latest top 3 diagnoses separated by `;`.

Avoid:
- Do not get stuck on overly detailed points before covering the main clinical picture.
- Do not repeat the same or very similar question, examination, or test request.
- If an examination or test result is unavailable, move on and try a different useful action.
- Do not ask the patient for information that was already provided unless clarification is necessary.

Use `respond` for the next hospital interaction. Do not provide a final diagnosis during this information-gathering phase.
",

highlights.title: Final OSCE note and differential diagnosis

patient_symptoms: Two days ago onset of low back pain after lifting a heavy object. Pain localized to the lower back; does not radiate to buttock or legs.

Location: central low back in the lumbar region, midline; sharp component and dull ache. Onset two days ago after lifting a heavy object. Persistent with no radiation to buttock/legs. Severity 6-7/10 at rest; up to 8/10 with bending or lifting. Mostly constant; worsens with movement; relieved by rest, heat, and mild analgesics.

Red flags: none; no fever, night sweats, weight loss, numbness/weakness in legs, bowel/bladder changes, gait disturbance, or night pain. Past history: no prior back problems; non-smoker, no alcohol; occupation: teacher with heavy lifting 2 days ago; OTC analgesics PRN; BMI not documented; family history negative for spine disease or cancer; pain midline lumbar region with sharp component and dull ache; 6-8/10; constant with movement; relieved by rest/heat.
Current pain medications: ibuprofen 400 mg Q6h PRN; acetaminophen 500 mg Q6h PRN; no known drug allergies or NSAIDs intolerance.
physical_exmination_findings: Posture/gait: Not documented. Inspection: No visible deformities or bruising. Palpation: Localized tenderness over lumbar spine; paraspinal region tenderness. ROM: Limited due to pain, esp forward flexion. Localized lumbar tenderness present. SLR: Not documented. Lower extremity neuro exam: Normal; no signs of nerve root compression.
test_results: X-ray lumbar spine: ordered; result pending.
Non-contrast lumbar spine MRI: test unavailable.

messages.content:
STATIC MEMORY
{
  "system_prompt": "You are an AgentClinic information-gathering clinician. Your goal is to collect enough clinical information to support diagnosis by interacting with the hospital environment and maintaining a concise OSCE note.

Use these tools:
- `update_osce_note`: update the OSCE note after every new patient response, physical examination result, or test result.
  - Use section `differential_diagnosis_list` for the current top 3 most likely diagnoses.
  - Format `differential_diagnosis_list` content as one string separated by semicolons, for example: `Acute appendicitis; Gastroenteritis; Ovarian torsion`.
- `bash`: call `src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py` for AgentClinic actions:
  - `respond`: interact with the hospital environment using one action: `ask_patient`, `request_physical_examination`, or `request_test`.

Call AgentClinic actions through the `bash` tool with `uv run python`. Use this command shape:

```bash
# Ask the patient one focused clinical question.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"ask_patient","message":"When did the abdominal pain start, and where is it located now?"}}'
```

```bash
# Request one clinically useful test from the hospital environment.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_test","message":"Complete blood count"}}'
```

```bash
# Request one focused physical examination result from the hospital environment.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_physical_examination","message":"Abdominal examination"}}'
```

Workflow:
1. Keep a working differential diagnosis in mind while asking questions and requesting exams/tests.
2. Ask clinically useful questions that build a comprehensive picture: chief concern, history of present illness, associated symptoms, negatives, past history, medications, allergies, family/social history, and relevant risk factors.
3. Request focused physical examinations and tests only when they can meaningfully narrow the differential or assess severity.
4. After each hospital environment response, immediately update the OSCE note with the new information.
5. When having enough context about the patient or the likely diagnoses change, update OSCE section `differential_diagnosis_list` with the latest top 3 diagnoses separated by `;`.

Avoid:
- Do not get stuck on overly detailed points before covering the main clinical picture.
- Do not repeat the same or very similar question, examination, or test request.
- If an examination or test result is unavailable, move on and try a different useful action.
- Do not ask the patient for information that was already provided unless clarification is necessary.

Use `respond` for the next hospital interaction. Do not provide a final diagnosis during this information-gathering phase.
",
  "memory_object": {
    "plan": [],
    "evidence": [],
    "osce_note": {
      "patient_demographics": [],
      "patient_medical_history": [],
      "patient_social_history": [],
      "patient_symptoms": [],
      "physical_exmination_findings": [],
      "test_results": [],
      "differential_diagnosis_list": ""
    }
  }
}

```bash
# Ask the patient one focused clinical question.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"ask_patient","message":"When did the abdominal pain start, and where is it located now?"}}'
```

```bash

uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_test","message":"Complete blood count"}}'
```

```bash

uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_physical_examination","message":"Abdominal examination"}}'
```

- Do not repeat the same or very similar question, examination, or test request.
- If an examination or test result is unavailable, move on and try a different useful action.
- Do not ask the patient for information that was already provided unless clarification is necessary.

Use `respond` for the next hospital interaction. Do not provide a final diagnosis during this information-gathering phase.

```bash
# Ask the patient one focused clinical question.
```

messages.content:
STATIC MEMORY
{
  "system_prompt": "You are an AgentClinic information-gathering clinician. Your goal is to collect enough clinical information to support diagnosis by interacting with the hospital environment and maintaining a concise OSCE note.

Use these tools:
- `update_osce_note`: update the OSCE note after every new patient response, physical examination result, or test result.
  - Use section `differential_diagnosis_list` for the current top 3 most likely diagnoses.
  - Format `differential_diagnosis_list` content as one string separated by semicolons, for example: `Acute appendicitis; Gastroenteritis; Ovarian torsion`.
- `bash`: call `src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py` for AgentClinic actions:
  - `respond`: interact with the hospital environment using one action: `ask_patient`, `request_physical_examination`, or `request_test`.

Call AgentClinic actions through the `bash` tool with `uv run python`. Use this command shape:

```bash
# Ask the patient one focused clinical question.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"ask_patient","message":"When did the abdominal pain start, and where is it located now?"}}'
```

```bash
# Request one clinically useful test from the hospital environment.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_test","message":"Complete blood count"}}'
```

```bash
# Request one focused physical examination result from the hospital environment.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_physical_examination","message":"Abdominal examination"}}'
```

Workflow:
1. Keep a working differential diagnosis in mind while asking questions and requesting exams/tests.
2. Ask clinically useful questions that build a comprehensive picture: chief concern, history of present illness, associated symptoms, negatives, past history, medications, allergies, family/social history, and relevant risk factors.
3. Request focused physical examinations and tests only when they can meaningfully narrow the differential or assess severity.
4. After each hospital environment response, immediately update the OSCE note with the new information.
5. When having enough context about the patient or the likely diagnoses change, update OSCE section `differential_diagnosis_list` with the latest top 3 diagnoses separated by `;`.

Avoid:
- Do not get stuck on overly detailed points before covering the main clinical picture.
- Do not repeat the same or very similar question, examination, or test request.
- If an examination or test result is unavailable, move on and try a different useful action.
- Do not ask the patient for information that was already provided unless clarification is necessary.

Use `respond` for the next hospital interaction. Do not provide a final diagnosis during this information-gathering phase.
",

    "evidence": [],

Severity 6-7/10 at rest; up to 8/10 with bending or lifting. Mostly constant; worsens with movement; relieved by rest, heat, and mild analgesics.",
        "Red flags: none; no fever, night sweats, weight loss, numbness/weakness in legs, bowel/bladder changes, gait disturbance, or night pain. Past history: no prior back problems; non-smoker, no alcohol; occupation: teacher with heavy lifting 2 days ago; OTC analgesics PRN; BMI not documented; family history negative for spine disease or cancer; pain midline lumbar region with sharp component and dull ache; 6-8/10; constant with movement; relieved by rest/heat."
      ],
      "physical_exmination_findings": [
        "Posture/gait: Not documented. Inspection: No visible deformities or bruising. Palpation: Localized tenderness over lumbar spine; paraspinal region tenderness. ROM: Limited due to pain, esp forward flexion. Localized lumbar tenderness present. SLR: Not documented. Lower extremity neuro exam: Normal; no signs of nerve root compression."
      ],
      "test_results": [
        "X-ray lumbar spine: ordered; result pending."
      ],
      "differential_diagnosis_list": "Acute nonspecific low back pain; Lumbar strain; Vertebral compression fracture"
    }
  }
}

messages.content:
STATIC MEMORY
{
  "system_prompt": "You are an AgentClinic information-gathering clinician. Your goal is to collect enough clinical information to support diagnosis by interacting with the hospital environment and maintaining a concise OSCE note.

Use these tools:
- `update_osce_note`: update the OSCE note after every new patient response, physical examination result, or test result.
  - Use section `differential_diagnosis_list` for the current top 3 most likely diagnoses.
  - Format `differential_diagnosis_list` content as one string separated by semicolons, for example: `Acute appendicitis; Gastroenteritis; Ovarian torsion`.
- `bash`: call `src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py` for AgentClinic actions:
  - `respond`: interact with the hospital environment using one action: `ask_patient`, `request_physical_examination`, or `request_test`.

Call AgentClinic actions through the `bash` tool with `uv run python`. Use this command shape:

```bash
# Ask the patient one focused clinical question.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"ask_patient","message":"When did the abdominal pain start, and where is it located now?"}}'
```

```bash
# Request one clinically useful test from the hospital environment.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_test","message":"Complete blood count"}}'
```

```bash
# Request one focused physical examination result from the hospital environment.
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"respond","content":{"action":"request_physical_examination","message":"Abdominal examination"}}'
```

Workflow:
1. Keep a working differential diagnosis in mind while asking questions and requesting exams/tests.
2. Ask clinically useful questions that build a comprehensive picture: chief concern, history of present illness, associated symptoms, negatives, past history, medications, allergies, family/social history, and relevant risk factors.
3. Request focused physical examinations and tests only when they can meaningfully narrow the differential or assess severity.
4. After each hospital environment response, immediately update the OSCE note with the new information.
5. When having enough context about the patient or the likely diagnoses change, update OSCE section `differential_diagnosis_list` with the latest top 3 diagnoses separated by `;`.

Avoid:
- Do not get stuck on overly detailed points before covering the main clinical picture.
- Do not repeat the same or very similar question, examination, or test request.
- If an examination or test result is unavailable, move on and try a different useful action.
- Do not ask the patient for information that was already provided unless clarification is necessary.

Use `respond` for the next hospital interaction. Do not provide a final diagnosis during this information-gathering phase.
",
  "memory_object": {
    "plan": [],
    "evidence": [],
    "osce_note": {
      "patient_demographics": [],
      "patient_medical_history": [],
      "patient_social_history": [],
      "patient_symptoms": [
        "Two days ago onset of low back pain after lifting a heavy object. Pain localized to the lower back; does not radiate to buttock or legs.",
        "Location: central low back in the lumbar region, midline; sharp component and dull ache. Onset two days ago after lifting a heavy object. Persistent with no radiation to buttock/legs. Severity 6-7/10 at rest; up to 8/10 with bending or lifting. Mostly constant; worsens with movement; relieved by rest, heat, and mild analgesics.",
        "Red flags: none; no fever, night sweats, weight loss, numbness/weakness in legs, bowel/bladder changes, gait disturbance, or night pain. Past history: no prior back problems; non-smoker, no alcohol; occupation: teacher with heavy lifting 2 days ago; OTC analgesics PRN; BMI not documented; family history negative for spine disease or cancer; pain midline lumbar region with sharp component and dull ache; 6-8/10; constant with movement; relieved by rest/heat.",
        "Current pain medications: ibuprofen 400 mg Q6h PRN; acetaminophen 500 mg Q6h PRN; no known drug allergies or NSAIDs intolerance."
      ],
      "physical_exmination_findings": [
        "Posture/gait: Not documented. Inspection: No visible deformities or bruising. Palpation: Localized tenderness over lumbar spine; paraspinal region tenderness. ROM: Limited due to pain, esp forward flexion. Localized lumbar tenderness present. SLR: Not documented. Lower extremity neuro exam: Normal; no signs of nerve root compression.",
        "SLR: Not documented/unavailable."
      ],
      "test_results": [
        "X-ray lumbar spine: ordered; result pending."
      ],
      "differential_diagnosis_list": "Acute nonspecific low back pain; Lumbar strain; Vertebral compression fracture"
    }
  }
}

messages.content:
STATIC MEMORY
{
  "system_prompt": "You are the final differential-diagnosis clinician in a two-phase AgentClinic
workflow. You have exactly one round. The information-seeking clinician has
already completed nine rounds and will provide `self.osce_note`, its current
`differential_diagnosis_list`, and the latest hospital response.

Use these tools:
- `update_evidence`: record the evidence used to compare candidate diagnoses.
  - Add supported and contradicting evidence from the supplied OSCE note and
    latest hospital response.
  - In `source`, cite the OSCE-note section or `latest hospital response`.
  - When applying medical knowledge that is not stated in the case, identify
    the source as `medical self-knowledge`.
- `bash`: call `src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py` to return the final diagnosis.

Workflow:
1. Read the complete supplied `self.osce_note`, including
   `differential_diagnosis_list`, and the latest hospital response.
2. Compare the leading candidates. Use `update_evidence` to record the key
   supporting and contradicting case evidence and the medical self-knowledge
   that connects that evidence to each candidate.
3. Select the single most likely diagnosis. Return only the diagnosis name as
   the `final_diagnosis` content; do not include explanation, alternatives,
   punctuation, or a confidence statement.
4. The final action must use the Bash tool with this command shape:


```bash
uv run python src/agent/agentclinic_tools.py '{"tool_name":"final_diagnosis","content":"Acute appendicitis"}'
```

",
  "memory_object": {
    "plan": [],
    "evidence": [],
    "osce_note": {
      "patient_demographics": [],
      "patient_medical_history": [],
      "patient_social_history": [],
      "patient_symptoms": [],
      "physical_exmination_findings": [],
      "test_results": [],
      "differential_diagnosis_list": ""
    }
  }
}

The appended agent trajectory follows as subsequent messages.

Patient: Two days ago I started having this pain after lifting a heavy object. It’s localized to my lower back and does not radiate to my buttock or legs.

Doctor: Please describe: exact location and quality of pain (e.g., dull, sharp, aching); onset and duration; any radiation to buttock or legs; pain severity 0-10; constant or intermittent; factors that worsen or relieve it (movement, rest, lifting).

Patient: Location and quality: central low back in the lumbar region, around the midline with a sharp component and a dull ache. Onset was two days ago after lifting a heavy object; the pain has been persistent with no radiation to buttock or legs. Severity is about 6-7/10 at rest and up to 8/10 with bending or lifting; it is mostly constant and worsens with movement, relieved by rest, heat, and mild analgesics.

Doctor: Please describe: (1) red flags (fever/night sweats, unintended weight loss, numbness/weakness in legs, bowel/bladder changes); (2) history of prior back problems, osteoporosis, cancer; (3) current medications and allergies; (4) smoking, alcohol, BMI; (5) occupation and any recent heavy lifting or prolonged awkward postures; (6) family history of spine disease or cancer; (7) any night pain or pain at rest; (8) any gait changes; (9) other factors you think are important.
